title: MP other-player beam-tick reads/writes player struct via raw N64 offsets + N64 hand stride (raw-cast cluster, filed)
suspect: src/game/bondview.c:25429 (hand-pos copy srcOff=i*936 N64 stride, player+0xB50/B54/B58)
repro: Split-screen / multi-player beam-tick for a non-current player (g_playerPointers[playerIndex]) reads and writes the player struct through raw N64 byte offsets and indexes hands[] with the N64 936-byte stride.

history.note: ASM-audit batch-6 raw-cast sweep -- the COMPLEX member of the class (filed, not fixed this pass). Unlike FID-0085/0087/0088/0091/0092 (single-field one-liners with a named-field sibling), this is a whole cluster in the MP other-player beam/aim tick (bondview.c ~24935-25439) that is NATIVE-ACTIVE (no #ifdef guard) yet uses N64 raw offsets throughout: aimback/aimsideback = player+0x2A00/0x2A04; chrSetFiring reads player+0x875 (hand0 firing) / player+0xC1D (hand1 firing); the 2-hand beam loop reads/writes player+handOffset+0x2A10/0x2A14/0x2A18 (beam pos) and player+handOffset4+0x2A28 (last-active frame); and the hand-position source uses srcOff = i*936 (the N64 struct hand stride, 968 on native) with player+srcOff+0xB50/0xB54/0xB58. On the 64-bit layout every one of these offsets (and the 936 stride) is wrong. It affects only the MP/split-screen non-current-player beam+aim path, which the current deterministic 1P oracle does not exercise, so it is left filed-with-derivation pending a per-field native mapping (each needs its named field or an offsetof-correct expression, and the i*936 index must become &hands[i].<field>). Derivation: docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0094-mp-beam-rawcast.md.

Root-caused against VERSION_US playerTickBeams ASM (7F08B688 aimback+0x2A00; 7F08BE40/BE54 firing lb player+0x875/+0xC1D; 7F08BEF0 hand-source lwc1 player+i*936+0xB50; beam pos +0x2A10, last-active +0x2A28). Native mapping: firing = hands[i].weapon_firing_status (gun.c:1949 accesses same field by name; raws one 936 stride apart 0xC1D-0x875); hand-source = hands[i].field_B50 with real stride 968 not 936; post-hands 0x2A00+ fields shifted by the uniform post-hands delta (FID-0088/0091/0092), so every raw lands on the wrong native field (and the 0x2A10 write corrupts the ptr_text_first_mp_award pointer bytes). Divergence machine-proven: added FID-0094 compile-time locks to test_struct_layout.c (offsetof != raw 0x875/0xC1D/0xB50; sizeof(hand) != 936) -- all hold. Code fix authored in derivation but NOT applied: only site is bondview.c playerTickBeams, off-limits this pass (raw-cast/determinism agent owns it; 80d64f1 explicitly deferred FID-0094). Ready-to-apply patch + split-screen gate in docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0094-mp-beam-rawcast.md.
